pattern: With the array this short, simply trying every possible starting position for the first of the two equal-length strictly increasing runs and directly checking both runs is fast enough and completely unambiguous.
hints: The second run always starts exactly k positions after the first one starts, so only the start of the first run needs to be chosen.
For a candidate start a, check that nums[a..a+k-1] is strictly increasing and, separately, that nums[a+k..a+2k-1] is strictly increasing.
Try every valid start a from 0 up to n - 2k and return true the moment one works; if none work, return false.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a matching pair exists in the middle
statement example, no matching pair at that k
smallest possible k, only one bit of room to spare
the whole array is one long increasing run, so any split works
a plateau breaks the run right where the second subarray would need to start
k equals exactly half the array length
decreasing overall but with two short increasing runs adjacent to each other

stress.description: random arrays and k values within the small constraint bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    max_k = max(1, n // 2)
    k = rng.randint(1, max_k)
    return [nums, k]
